title: Extract shared anthropic-tls/oauth/aliasing crate between mu and claude-proxy (deps-drift hygiene)
description:
## Trigger

2026-05-19 dependabot scan surfaced 2 open alerts on claude-proxy (1 HIGH + 1 MEDIUM, both `rust-openssl` UB/heap-overflow CVEs in TLS/X509 code paths). mu has 0 open alerts. Investigation revealed the cause:

| Repo | reqwest features | TLS backend | openssl in dep tree? |
|---|---|---|---|
| mu | `default-features = false, features = ["rustls-tls", ...]` | rustls | no |
| claude-proxy | `features = ["stream", "json"]` (keeps defaults) | native-tls | yes (rust-openssl) |

So the alerts on claude-proxy are real, mu was never vulnerable to them, and the divergence is purely **configuration drift between two repos that talk to the same upstream (api.anthropic.com)**. The immediate fix is a one-line reqwest config swap (filed as a separate PR). This bead is for the structural fix: prevent the drift from re-occurring.

## What

Create a thin shared crate (working name `anthropic-tls-client` or similar) that both mu and claude-proxy depend on for the bits they currently re-implement in parallel:

1. **reqwest configuration** — TLS backend choice, common features, optional timeouts/connection-pool defaults. Today's drift could be a `pub use reqwest;` re-export with the agreed feature set pinned in one place.
2. **OAuth helpers** — claude-proxy holds OAuth credentials for claude_personal + claude_work; mu has OAuth flow for OpenAI Codex (and possibly claude.ai in the future). The token-refresh / file-storage / expired-token-on-first-request lazy-reload pattern is duplicated logic.
3. **Model aliasing** — claude-proxy's `[backends.model_map]` (`claude-opus-4-7` → `anthropic/claude-opus-4.7` for openrouter) is the kind of canonical mapping multiple consumers will want. mu has its own provider abstraction; some overlap.
4. **Failover / retry-with-backoff** — claude-proxy has `[failover].order` + `triggers = [500, 502, 503, 504, 429, 529]`. Anyone consuming the Anthropic API benefits from this. Today every consumer re-implements (or skips) it.

Start with #1 (reqwest config — actually trivial, just a workspace dep) and only extend as drift becomes evident. Don't over-engineer.

## Why P3 (not P2 / P1)

- Immediate openssl alerts are fixed by a one-line PR (separate); P0/P1 urgency was for that
- This bead is hygiene: it prevents the NEXT divergence, not today's
- Library extraction has a cost; should only happen when ≥2 specific divergences are documented, not speculatively
- Acceptable to defer for weeks while the immediate fix lands and other priorities ship

## Acceptance criteria (proposed; refine when starting)

- [ ] Shared crate exists (location TBD: new repo? path-dep? part of mu workspace?)
- [ ] mu's `Cargo.toml` reqwest line is replaced with a dep on the shared crate
- [ ] claude-proxy's `Cargo.toml` reqwest line is the same
- [ ] Both repos pass full test suites after the swap (no behavior change)
- [ ] A dependabot alert that hits the shared crate's deps fixes both consumers via one update

## Cross-refs

- claude-proxy-d8q: this session's other bead (proxy health-check + graceful-degradation gaps) — same outage thread, different layer
- Separate PR (filed alongside this bead): one-line reqwest feature swap to close the two immediate openssl alerts on claude-proxy
- mu commit `3314924d7674` (chore(deps): bump oauth2 4.4→5 and ratatui 0.28→0.30 — closes 4 dependabot alerts) — different vulnerability surface (rustls-webpki) but illustrates the kind of cascading dep upgrade that a shared crate would amortize across consumers

## Open design questions

- Where does the shared crate live? Possibilities:
  - New public_github repo `anthropic-rust-shared` (clean separation)
  - Path-dep inside mu's workspace (zero infra; couples lifecycle to mu)
  - Path-dep inside a new `~/src/public_github/anthropic-rust-shared` (workspace, dual ownership)
- Does this become more important if a third Rust consumer of api.anthropic.com appears (some future agent-router rewrite, etc.)?

Don't answer these until starting; today's purpose is just to record the design space.

title: Service health check + graceful per-backend startup degradation (2026-05-19 outage)
description:
## Background

2026-05-19 14:00 EDT — claude-proxy was running as a FreeBSD service (`service claude_proxy status` reported green for ~23 hours) but the inner binary had been crash-looping every ~1 second the entire time. Result: nothing listened on 127.0.0.1:3180 for 23 hours; every claude --print, pi --provider anthropic, and mu serve --provider anthropic call across both the orchestrator and any sub-agents got ECONNREFUSED-disguised-as-180s-ECONNRESET. The session-level impact was significant: roughly 5 hours of failed-dispatch debugging on the claude-personal account before the diagnosis surfaced.

Root cause: `/home/tcovert/.claude/.credentials.json` (the claude_work backend's credentials) was root-owned (likely from an overnight cron). The proxy runs as tcovert (per `claude_proxy_user` in rc.conf) and got "Permission denied" trying to read it. Since startup loads ALL configured backends and any failure is fatal, the proxy crashed and `daemon -r` respawned it for 23h.

Fix applied: `sudo chown tcovert:tcovert /home/tcovert/.claude/.credentials.json && sudo service claude_proxy restart`. claude_work also had an expired token, but the proxy correctly lazy-refreshes on first request — that's not the bug.

## Two related improvements

### 1. Real health check in the rc.d service status path

`service claude_proxy status` currently only checks the `daemon(8)` wrapper's pidfile. The wrapper survives indefinitely while the inner binary crash-loops. Today's gap is that no signal told the operator about a 23-hour outage. Even an extended outage on api.anthropic.com wouldn't produce this symptom (different error shape).

Concrete options (any one would have caught today's incident):
- **Listen probe**: rc.d `status_cmd` checks `sockstat -4l | grep ':3180 '` (or equivalent netstat). Cheap, no IPC into the proxy.
- **Mgmt API health endpoint**: claude-proxy already exposes `127.0.0.1:3181` for mgmt; add `GET /health` returning 200 if listener is bound + at least one backend loaded successfully. status_cmd probes that.
- **Log-rate monitor**: separate watchdog that tail's `/var/log/claude_proxy.log` and alerts if "Permission denied" or other ERROR rate exceeds N/sec for M seconds. Heavier, more general.

Recommend the listen probe — it's the smallest change with the highest signal. The mgmt API health endpoint is the next step if more nuance is wanted (e.g., distinguish "listening but no backends" from "fully healthy").

### 2. Graceful degradation on per-backend startup failure

Today's outage would not have happened at all if claude-proxy treated per-backend startup failures as `WARN + skip` instead of `ERROR + crash`. The claude_work backend hasn't been actively used in weeks (operator noted: basic plan, no opus, runs out of tokens quickly). It being unreadable should not have taken down claude_personal + openrouter.

Proposed semantics:
- For each backend in `[[backends]]`, attempt to load credentials at startup.
- On failure: log `WARN backend '<name>' startup failed: <error>; will retry on first request OR mark unavailable`. Continue loading other backends.
- The failover order (currently `[claude_personal, claude_work, openrouter]`) already gives the right runtime semantics: if `claude_work` is the second in the chain and it's unavailable, requests fall through to `openrouter`. The startup just needs to mirror this tolerance.
- Hard failure should be reserved for "no backends configured" or "all backends failed to load AND the proxy can't serve anything."

This change alone would have made today's failure mode: a WARN at startup, otherwise functional, recovered fully when ownership was fixed. The health-check (#1) becomes the diagnostic catch for the residual case where ALL backends fail.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `service claude_proxy status` detects a non-listening inner binary (any mechanism is fine; listen probe preferred for simplicity).
- [ ] Failed credential read for one backend logs WARN but does not crash the proxy.
- [ ] Failover still works correctly when the failed-at-startup backend is in the order list (route past it).
- [ ] Failed backend recovers on next `POST /reload` (mgmt API) without proxy restart.
- [ ] Test: with claude_work creds unreadable, claude_personal requests succeed.

## Cross-refs

- Diagnostic memory: agent memory `claude_proxy_dead_crash_loop_2026_05_19`
- Session impact: agent memory `iter_8_review_self_dispatch_fallback_2026_05_19` (56ac0900)
- Operating note: claude_work backend can be safely removed from `~/.config/claude-proxy/config.toml` since it's not actively used; that's a config-side workaround, not a code-side fix.

## Why P2 (not P1)

- The trigger (root-owned creds file) is rare and currently invisible — overnight cron is the likely cause but hasn't been identified
- Workaround is documented (chown + restart) and takes <30s once diagnosed
- The 23h failure window today was bad, but partly because the wedge wasn't recognized until the operator hypothesized "both pointing at the proxy and the proxy is stuck"
- Either fix in isolation would prevent a repeat; together they make a repeat impossible
